title: Holographic calibration
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bp3j5","text":"This is the standard protocol for 2-photon holographic calibration with In vivo Ultima Bruker microscope through GRIN lens used for Rodrigues-Vaz and Athalye et al, 2025.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7k3d5","text":"-2-photon lasers (imaging and stimulation) and Pockels cell controller were turned on.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"2rov7","text":"-Wear appropriate PPE as required by your institution. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"c3p55","text":"•\tFluorescent slide","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3k6jk","text":"•\tDistilled water ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"131rc","text":"•\t2-photon laser scanning microscope with spatial light modulator module (Ultima In Vivo, Bruker)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6jpb6","text":"•\tFemtosecond Ti:saphire laser (Chameleon Vision-S, Coherent)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"632lg","text":"•\tLow repetition photostimulation laser (Monaco 1035-40, Coherent)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Confirm imaging laser and photostimulation laser are aligned with target at objective output.
Burn single spot and confirm calibration worked. If not, repeat step 4 as many times as necessary.
Follow PrairieView software’s calibration routine to ensure alignment between photostimulation and imaging lasers: burn spots
Focus on top of the sample.
Place fluorescent sample under 16X water-immersion Nikon objective.
Measure power of stimulation laser at the 16X water-immersion Nikon objective output. This was done at least once at beginning of each cohort.
creator: Ines Rodrigues-Vaz
